div.head: The Minister in Switzerland (Wilson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 262. Department’s 160, June 13, 6 p.m. I appreciate the full information which your telegram gives me.

Copies of all telegrams sent and received on this and other miscellaneous subjects are exchanged between the Delegation and Consulate so we are thus always continually informed of each other’s reports as well as instructions. In addition there is oral communication between the Delegation and the Consulate if there is any doubt with regard to the despatch of a particular message to the Department. For example, I read over my 261, June 12, 11 a.m., to Gilbert before telegraphing it to ensure that there was no crossing of wires.

After reading the previous correspondence both from and to the Consulate and the Delegation and discussion of the matter with Gilbert I have decided not to transmit any information to Najera at this stage.
